query: What is the J.P. Morgan view on the speed of the energy transition, and what are the key findings from their 2025 annual energy paper?
scope_constraints: Focus on transition speed, costs, policy, and technology outlook

expected_report_type.expected_time_horizon: Decades (energy transition) with near-term focus on 2025 policies and deployments
expected_report_type.target_reader: High-net-worth private bank clients, institutional investors, portfolio managers

institution.name: J.P. Morgan Private Bank / Wealth Management

report_title: Eye on the Market: Heliocentrism — 15th Annual Energy Paper

source_pack.name: EI Statistical Review of World Energy, JPMAM 2024
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: Goldman Sachs Carbonomics, October 2024
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: Lauri Holappa, Aalto University, March 2019; Boston Metal, May 2022
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: NASA, NOAA, EPA, University of Hawaii SOEST data on climate metrics
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: WWF/Zoological Society of London Vertebrate Biodiversity Index
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management grid optimization model
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

key_facts.claim: The renewable share of final energy consumption is advancing at a linear pace of 0.3%–0.6% per year globally, and 0.6% per year even in Europe, the world's transition leader.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Refer to the chart titled 'Decarbonization is a linear industrial transition' in the executive summary (page 1).
key_facts.fact: The renewable share of final energy consumption is advancing at a linear pace of 0.3%–0.6% per year globally, and 0.6% per year even in Europe, the world's transition leader.

key_facts.claim: Global spending of US$9 trillion since 2010 on wind, solar, EVs, storage, electrified heat, and power grids has only achieved this linear pace.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Stated in the first paragraph of the Executive Summary.
key_facts.fact: Global spending of US$9 trillion since 2010 on wind, solar, EVs, storage, electrified heat, and power grids has only achieved this linear pace.

key_facts.claim: Goldman Sachs, previously aggressive in renewable transition forecasts, struck a more cautious tone in October 2024, now assuming a longer life for hydrocarbon assets and growing natural gas demand until 2050.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Check footnote 1 on page 1 of the report, titled 'Reality Bites'.
key_facts.fact: Goldman Sachs, previously aggressive in renewable transition forecasts, struck a more cautious tone in October 2024, now assuming a longer life for hydrocarbon assets and growing natural gas demand until 2050.

key_facts.claim: The report describes the 'wilting prospects for the hydrogen economy' and multiple green hydrogen project cancellations.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: See section titled 'Frydrogen' in the Table of Contents and page 43.
key_facts.fact: The report describes the 'wilting prospects for the hydrogen economy' and multiple green hydrogen project cancellations.

key_facts.claim: Michael Cembalest is the Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management and sole author of this 15th annual energy paper.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Author credit on title page and header of each page.
key_facts.fact: Michael Cembalest is the Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management and sole author of this 15th annual energy paper.

key_facts.claim: The report includes a proprietary grid optimization model analyzing the cost and configuration of deeply decarbonized US grids.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Section listed in Table of Contents: 'Our grid optimization model' (page 36).
key_facts.fact: The report includes a proprietary grid optimization model analyzing the cost and configuration of deeply decarbonized US grids.

key_facts.claim: The paper covers satellite-based methane tracking from US gas basins, questioning the accuracy of bottom-up inventories.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Section 'Space Mountain' in Table of Contents and page 43.
key_facts.fact: The paper covers satellite-based methane tracking from US gas basins, questioning the accuracy of bottom-up inventories.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Stating that the energy transition is accelerating without noting the linear trend argued by the report
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Referring to Goldman Sachs' October 2024 view as unchanged from earlier aggressive transition forecasts
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Claiming that hydrogen economy prospects are robust without mentioning the documented project cancellations
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Attributing the Los Angeles fires solely to climate change without noting the report's multi-factor analysis
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Omitting the $9 trillion cumulative spending figure when discussing the transition's speed
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Describing the European decarbonization experience as low-cost or without tradeoffs
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

prohibited_mistakes.mistake: Ignoring the author's skeptical stance on a near-term OECD nuclear renaissance
prohibited_mistakes.why_it_matters: This would materially reduce benchmark answer quality.

evaluation_hooks.factual_accuracy: Verify key data claims from original sources (EI Statistical Review, Goldman Sachs). Check that the linear transition argument is not misrepresented as an acceleration.
evaluation_hooks.source_fidelity: Ensure the report's skeptical tone and nuanced findings (e.g., LA fires multifaceted, nuclear renaissance yet to materialize) are preserved.
evaluation_hooks.completeness: Confirm that all major sections from the table of contents are covered, especially executive summary, grid model, hydrogen, methane, and aviation fuels.

reference_notes.author_role: Chairman of Market and Investment Strategy for J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management
reference_notes.unique_disclaimer: Objects may be further away than they appear
reference_notes.tone_summary: Authoritative, data-heavy, skeptical of hype, acknowledging complexity and non-linear risks
